topic_title: Algorithms in everyday life — recipes, GPS routes, sorting queues
verdict: PASS with 1 soft finding. All hard checks pass. Soft finding: word count (2,850) is ~5% below the standard profile floor of 3,000 words — not a hard fail but author may wish to expand Core Concepts or Implementation by ~150 words to meet the floor. No forward references, no scope creep, no readability issues, primary resource cited, citation density well above 60%.

density_check.note: Nearly all factual paragraphs in sections 5, 6, and 7 end with at least one citation marker ([1], [2], [3]). Tables are anchored by citations in immediately surrounding prose. No uncited claim blocks detected.

sequence_drift.note: All technical terms used are either introduced in topics 1.1–1.9 or in this topic's own concepts_introduced list (recipe as algorithm, route-finding, sorting algorithm, comparison rule, stopping condition, tie-breaking rule). 'Determinism' is correctly attributed back to topic 1.2 with an explicit pointer. No named future concepts used without a 'covered later' pointer. No CS theory terms (Big-O, graph, node, edge, Dijkstra, bubble sort) appear. Email routing mentioned in Real-World Patterns as a one-sentence analogy, not defined or taught.

length_sanity.finding: Word count 2,850 is approximately 5% below the standard profile floor of 3,000 words. This is a soft finding only (count is above the hard-fail floor of 1,500). Recommend expanding Core Concepts or Implementation by ~150 words to meet the 3,000-word floor.

topic_scope.note: Content stays within the headline scope: recipes, GPS routes, and sorting queues as algorithm examples. No Big-O notation, no code implementation, no algorithmic complexity, no CS data structure theory. Section 6 (Implementation) applies the three domains practically and is within scope. Section 7 (Real-World Patterns) names email routing and content delivery servers in one-sentence analogies without defining or teaching them. No deferred concept is bolded or given a defining clause.

undefined_terms.note: 'Route-finding' defined inline at first use (section 5.2). 'Sorting algorithm' defined inline at first use (section 5.3). 'Algorithm' correctly reused from topic 1.9 without re-definition. 'Determinism' attributed to topic 1.2 with an explicit pointer. No unexplained acronyms.

jargon_dump.note: No single paragraph introduces 3 or more new terms simultaneously. New terms are introduced one per section (route-finding in 5.2, sorting algorithm in 5.3).

academic_register.note: Prose is active voice, addressed to 'you', concrete before abstract throughout. No hedging phrases detected. Sentence lengths are within target range.

soft_findings_summary.finding: Word count 2,850 is ~5% below the standard profile floor of 3,000 words. Recommend adding ~150 words to Core Concepts or Implementation. Not a hard fail.
